title: Data Classification Policy
body: This policy defines data classification levels: Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted. All data must be labeled accordingly. Handling procedures vary by classification level. Confidential and Restricted data must be encrypted in transit and at rest.

title: Incident Response Policy
body: In the event of a security incident, the incident response team must be notified immediately. The incident should be documented and classified according to severity. Containment and eradication steps should follow the defined playbooks. Post-incident review is mandatory.

title: Secret Handling Policy
body: This policy governs the handling of secrets within the organization. All secrets must be stored in the approved secure vault system. Plaintext secrets are strictly prohibited in any repository, configuration file, or communication channel. Developers must use the vault client to retrieve secrets at runtime and never hard-code credentials. Regular audits will be conducted to ensure compliance. Violations may result in disciplinary action.

title: Access Control Policy
body: Access to systems and data is granted based on the principle of least privilege. Role-based access control (RBAC) is enforced across all applications. Access reviews are performed quarterly. Temporary access must be approved and documented.
